title: Completion semantics evolved from artifact existence to evidence-gated acceptance

observed: The canonical queue marks work complete when declared required evidence is satisfied, not merely when source artifacts exist.
Recent continuity work introduced fail-closed reconciliation for unsupported completion claims and separated canonical state, append-only records, derived projections, and external behavioral proof.
Recent frontier and Cloudflare commits continue to distinguish deterministic source tests from deployed or device-level proof.
inferred: Across the project, the operational meaning of complete has evolved into a layered evidence contract: artifact created, source verified, deployment or runtime identity established, and externally relevant behavior observed where required.
This evolution is a project-wide decision pattern rather than a single-team implementation detail.
proposed: Represent completion as a vector of evidence dimensions instead of a single boolean: artifact, source_test, provenance, deployment, runtime, and external_observation.
Derived queue projections should expose which dimensions are satisfied and must preserve unresolved status when any declared required dimension is absent.
superseded: A committed file or passing isolated test alone is sufficient to claim end-to-end completion.
unresolved: The repository does not yet have a shared schema that maps each queue item's free-text required_evidence entries onto standardized evidence dimensions.
No authorized canonical-promotion process has yet been defined for converting append-only completion records into shared queue state.

provenance.review_route: Falsify by identifying an accepted queue completion that lacks one or more evidence dimensions explicitly required by its own queue item.
